instruction:
Find the value of the integral
$$
I=\int_{0}^{1} \frac{\operatorname{ln} (1-x)}{x} \mathrm{d} x.
$$.

Options:
A. $$
\pi / 4
$$
B. $$
-1 / 2
$$
C. $$
\pi^{2} / 8
$$
D. $$
- \pi^{2} / 6
$$
E. $$
- \ln(3/2)
$$
F. $$
0
$$
G. $$
e^{-\pi}/2
$$
H. $$
\ln 2
$$
I. $$
\sqrt{2} / 3
$$
J. $$
1 - \ln \pi
$$
